tool.name: kraken-biom
tool.description: Create BIOM-format tables from Kraken output
tool.command:
#import os #import re
## 1. Convert input files to symlinks based on sample names, stripping dataset extensions #for $file in $input_files: #set $identifier = $file.element_identifier #set $base, $ext = os.path.splitext(str($identifier)) #if $ext.lower() in ['.tabular', '.tsv', '.txt', '.dat', '.csv', '.report']: #set $identifier = $base #end if #set $safename = re.sub('[^\w\-_\.]', '_', str($identifier)) #set $safename = $safename + '.report' ln -s '$file' '$safename' && #end for
## 2. Run kraken-biom with selected options kraken-biom *.report #if $max_rank: --max '$max_rank' #end if #if $min_rank: --min '$min_rank' #end if --fmt '$fmt' $gzip #if $metadata: --metadata '$metadata' #end if -o '$output1'

param.name: input_files
param.label: Kraken report output
param.help: Select taxonomy classification report files produced by Kraken.

param.name: max_rank
param.label: Maximum taxonomic rank
param.help: Write BIOM tables with taxonomic data up to this rank.

param.name: min_rank
param.label: Minimum taxonomic rank
param.help: Write BIOM tables with taxonomic data down to this rank.

param.label: Output format
param.help: Specify the file format for the output BIOM table.

param.label: Gzip compression
param.help: Compress the output table with gzip (only applicable for JSON/TSV formats).

param.label: Sample metadata file
param.help: Optional metadata file in TSV or Tabular format. The first column must contain the sample IDs (matching the input file names without extensions).

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**What it does**
The program takes as input, one or more files output from the kraken-report tool. Each file is parsed and the counts for each OTU (operational taxonomic unit) are recorded, along with database ID (e.g. NCBI), and lineage. The extracted data are then stored in a BIOM table where each count is linked to the Sample and OTU it belongs to. Sample IDs are extracted from the input filenames (everything up to the '.').
